expected_effect: Improve calm and low-retail competitiveness without reopening broad arb leakage.
expected_failure_signature: If calm windows cheapen both sides together or arb_loss_to_retail_gain regresses, the split still behaves like a replay.
expected_win_condition: Recover weak calm slices while keeping one-sided toxicity protection selective and defensive by default.
hidden_coupling_removed: The incumbent uses one additive surface to set both the symmetric defensive floor and the toxic-side surcharge.
rationale: Replace the single additive quote spine with a persistent symmetric baseline floor plus a separate side-specific protection channel so calm competitiveness no longer requires broad cheapening.
title: Baseline split with one-sided protection
why_not_coefficient_retune: This changes quote assembly into two explicit channels instead of retuning one shared surface.
